results.title: Ankali means friend
results.brief_description: Ankali is part of Sydney’s Albion Street AIDS Centre. It is run by professional staff and 145 volunteer counsellors. The Ankali project has, since 1985, offered emotional support to people with AIDS. This film focuses on two volunteer counsellors, a psychologist and a teacher, and their clients.

results.title: Shoppingtown
results.creator_credit: David Caesar, Peter Leovic and AFTRS
results.brief_description: Short Australian film by David Caesar which creates a portrait of Parramatta Shoppingtown in August 1986. Initial blank screen isolates people’s words as they voice their aspirations and understanding of happiness. Silence follows as a series of film portraits reveal the inhabitants of Shoppingtown, a shopping centre in suburban Sydney. A rapid montage of images and sounds, most prominently the sounds of the checkout scanner, denote the commercial aspect of a space which has been established in the film as communal and civic. The portraits of shoppers and mall dwellers return, ending in a portrait of the filmmakers themselves.

results.title: Affordable Housing: Dream or Reality
results.brief_description: With changing lifestyles and the enormous cost of buying a freestanding home, this video looks at basic alternative design elements in housing and town planning to accommodate both aesthetically.

results.title: By design?
results.creator_credit: Diane O'Connor and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Provides a behind the scene look at the world of fashion. People from Cherry Lane fashion house, “Dolly” and “Mode” magazines and the Australian Wool corporation talk about what is involved in predicting fashions trends, designing, producing garments and putting together fashion.

results.title: Here's to your health: skincare
results.creator_credit: Craig Reardon, Robert Grant and Victoria. Dept. of Health. Health Promotion Unit
results.brief_description: Australia has the highest rate of skin cancer in the world. This program looks at the problem of skin cancer, how to detect it and how to care for your skin when out in the sun.

results.title: Feed them to the cannibals!
results.creator_credit: Fiona Cunningham Reid, Baobab Productions and Dangerous to Know
results.brief_description: In 1972 a small number of lesbians and gay men held the first Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras in Sydney, a then militant solidarity march for gay rights. Over the last two decades the Sydney Gay Mardi Gras has become one of the biggest festivals around the world, attracting hundreds of thousands of international and domestic tourists every year. The film explores the history of the Sydney lesbian and gay community, a community organised around relationships of love, solidarity and pleasure and which is challenging the traditional ways society has understood family, love and commitment. Many of the activists interviewed discuss the changes in gay and lesbian politics over the last twenty years and the changes in the lived experiences of non-heterosexuality.

results.title: Building an african village
results.brief_description: A short program which outlines a Community Aid Abroad project. Victorian school children learn from a group of visiting Africans the skills of mud brick building. Designed to promote positive images of Africa and its people.

results.title: Living with Aids
results.creator_credit: Shayne Mooney and Australia. Special Broadcasting Service
results.brief_description: Shayne Mooney interviews four men with two things in common - they are all gay and HIV positive. Two of the men, Colin (27), a photographer, and Ian, were filmed in the final stages of their lives. Peter Charlton and Chris Carter, political researcher, were HIV positive but had not then developed AIDS. The four men discuss how the illness has affected their lives, the discrimination they have experienced and how they have come to terms with the prospect of death with the help of AIDS support workers. It is an inspirational program that shows the triumph of the human spirit over suffering and death.

results.title: Keeping animals in the classroom
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Ivan Gaal and Curriculum Branch, Ministry of Education for Zoo Education Service
results.brief_description: Trevor Lunn introduces Karen Svalesen from the Zoo Education Service who examines the problems that can be encountered when keeping animals in the classroom. Suggests suitable animals and information sources to assist with animal care.

results.title: Bunny
results.creator_credit: Edwin James Lynch and Philip Ellery
results.brief_description: In this engaging short drama, Jane is convinced that her dog has killed the neighbours’ pet rabbit! In fact, the rabbit died of natural causes and was given a decent burial…. Jane deals with the problem with the naive resourcefulness of a young girl, much to the horror of her neighbours!
